description: Society, policy, history, languages, development and public-impact careers.

careers.name: Sociologist
careers.overview: Sociologist studies people, society, culture, policy, history, language and social change to solve human problems.
careers.whyChoose: Sociologist is useful because it builds strong thinking, writing, research and public-impact skills for diverse professional pathways.
careers.eligibility: Class 12 in any stream; Humanities subjects are useful but not always compulsory.
careers.educationPath: Class 12 -> BA/BSW/BPA or related degree -> Internship/research/projects -> Public, social or policy role.
careers.workEnvironment: Universities, NGOs, think tanks, media teams, policy organizations, government bodies and research firms.
careers.futureScope: Strong for policy, development, research, communication, civil services and human-centered organizations.
careers.aiImpact: AI will support research and drafting while interpretation, judgement and field context remain human-led.
cons: Early career may need portfolio or training
